# Unfog — Zero-Budget Growth Playbook (faceless / auto-generated video)

Goal: acquire ADHD users for €0, without you appearing on camera or filming anything.
The engine is **faceless short-form video** (TikTok + Instagram Reels + YouTube Shorts),
posted daily, plus **the product marketing itself** (shareable cards).

> Responsible-positioning rule (non-negotiable): Unfog is a **planner**, never medical
> advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Never say "cure", never imply a diagnosis. This keeps
> us out of trouble AND builds trust in a space full of misinformation.

---

## 1. The core insight from the research

- **#ADHDtok is enormous** and drives real installs. What performs: *relatable* content,
  gentle humor, "POV / it me" framing — not polished ads.
- **The product is the ad.** Finch went viral because users *screenshot and share their pet*.
  Our equivalent: shareable "my day, mapped" and "my week of wins" cards (P0 backlog item).
- **Faceless works.** You never need your face or voice — AI voiceover + text-on-screen +
  stock/animated b-roll is a proven, sustainable format.

---

## 2. The faceless video pipeline (all free tiers)

**Fastest path (script → finished video in minutes):**
1. **InVideo AI** (free tier) or **CapCut** (free): paste a script, it generates scenes,
   AI voiceover, captions, music. Export 9:16.
2. **Voice:** InVideo/CapCut built-in, or **ElevenLabs** free tier for a warmer narrator.
3. **Captions:** always on, big, word-by-word highlight (CapCut auto-captions). Non-negotiable
   for ADHD viewers who watch muted.
4. **Music:** use the platform's trending-sounds library *inside* TikTok/Reels (using a
   trending sound is itself a reach boost).

**Auto-pilot path (posts for you daily):** tools like **Faceless.video / AutoShorts / AutoReels**
generate + schedule + auto-post to all three platforms. Use one *only after* you've validated
2–3 hooks manually — automation amplifies whatever's working, it doesn't find it.

**Recommended weekly rhythm:** batch 7 scripts on Sunday (Claude writes them — see §4),
generate all 7 in one CapCut/InVideo session, schedule 1/day across TikTok + Reels + Shorts.
Same video, 3 platforms = 3x reach for 1x effort.

---

## 3. Content pillars (rotate these)

1. **"It me" relatability** (60%): name the ADHD pain. "POV: you have 11 tabs open in your
   brain and can't start any of them." End on the app as the exhale.
2. **Micro-demos** (25%): 8-second screen-capture of a brain dump becoming a calm plan, or the
   🌶️ tiny-steps slider turning "clean the kitchen" into 12 doable steps.
3. **Gentle education** (15%): one ADHD-friendly tip (body doubling, time blindness, the
   2-minute rule) — value first, app mentioned lightly.

Always end with a **soft CTA**: "link in bio if your brain needs this too." Never hard-sell.

---

## 4. 10 ready-to-film hooks (first line = the scroll-stopper)

1. "POV: your to-do list has 30 things and your brain picked *none* of them."
2. "The app I wish existed when I got diagnosed at 29."
3. "Watch me turn a brain-dump of chaos into a calm day in 10 seconds."
4. "'Clean the kitchen' is not one task. It's 14. Here's proof." (spiciness demo)
5. "Normal planners: RED. OVERDUE. Unfog: 'not now is a valid answer.'"
6. "I don't need a planner that shames me. I need one that hands me *one* thing."
7. "Time blindness is real. This is the only timer that helped." (focus demo)
8. "Your streak doesn't break when you rest here." (Wins demo)
9. "Made for brains that plan the day at 2am and forget it by 9." (energy-aware plan demo)
10. "3 ADHD apps everyone recommends — and the free one nobody talks about." (listicle, us last)

> Ask Claude any time: *"write me 7 faceless TikTok scripts for Unfog using pillars X, in
> this voice, 20–25s each, with on-screen text + voiceover + shot list."* You never write from scratch.

---

## 5. Distribution beyond video (still €0)

- **Reddit**: r/ADHD, r/ADHDwomen, r/adhd_programmers, r/productivity — *never* drop a link
  cold. Answer "what app do you use to start tasks?" threads with genuine help; mention Unfog
  as one option. Karma first, links second.
- **Build in public** on X/Threads: share the journey, the numbers, the design choices.
  Founders documenting an ADHD app *for* ADHD people is inherently followable.
- **Communities**: ADHD Discords/Facebook groups — participate, don't spam.
- **The waitlist → beta → testimonials → more content** loop: every happy beta user is a
  quote, a screenshot, a mini-case-study that becomes 3 more videos.

---

## 6. What to measure (weekly, be honest)

| Metric | Why it matters | Healthy signal |
|---|---|---|
| Landing → waitlist % | Does the promise land? | > 25–30% |
| **Founding (paid intent) / waitlist %** | Real willingness to pay | any meaningful # beats 100 free emails |
| Video → profile visits | Is the hook working? | iterate hooks weekly on the winners |
| Day-7 retention (beta) | The make-or-break number | ADHD-app top quartile ≈ 12–18% |

The Contrarian's rule: **if day-7 retention and paid-intent are both weak after a real
cohort, we change the product or the plan — no ego, no sunk cost.** That discipline is how
this becomes money instead of a hobby.

---

## 7. First 7 days (do this)
1. Set the AI key so demos show real AI plans.
2. Turn on notifications (retention) + keep the founding CTA live (validation).
3. Batch + schedule 7 faceless videos (hooks above).
4. Post 1/day to TikTok + Reels + Shorts. Reply to every comment (algorithm loves it).
5. Help in 3 Reddit threads. Watch the waitlist + founding numbers. Report back.
